publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Checked and unchecked exception design; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Checked and unchecked exception design: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Checked means serious and unchecked means harmless, so every important failure should extend Exception directly.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Choose checked or unchecked from caller recovery and contract needs, not severity; document the failure policy and avoid exceptions for routine alternatives.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Choose checked or unchecked from caller recovery and contract needs, not severity; document the failure policy and avoid exceptions for routine alternatives.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.
